title: Commentary on Investments: As of March 31, 2026
content:
Total investments declined to $16,874 as of March 31, 2026, reflecting a -3.5% decrease from the previous period. The portfolio remains conservatively structured and continues to be heavily weighted toward available-for-sale debt securities, which total $16,566 and represent virtually the entire investment portfolio. This holding type provides stable income.

Other investments totaled $302 and remain immaterial relative to the overall portfolio. Time deposits of $5 are also present but negligible in scale. The decline in total investments is primarily driven by a -3.6% decrease in available-for-sale debt securities, which strengthens liquidity management while maintaining an efficient earning asset base.

The investment maturity structure remains well distributed across multiple time horizons, supporting effective liquidity management and interest rate risk control. This mix includes buckets ranging from less than one year to over ten years, providing flexibility for the credit union's balance-sheet needs without exposing it to excessive duration mismatch risks.

Overall, Clearpath's investment portfolio remains conservative, liquid, and aligned with policy guidelines. Management continues to emphasize capital preservation, stable yield generation, and prudent duration management while maintaining flexibility to respond to changing balance-sheet needs and market opportunities.
